clusters.title: Python for post-run evaluation analysis and error clustering
clusters.summary: Captures discuss using Python notebooks for analysis tasks like clustering failure cases, generating confusion matrices, and mining patterns, strictly after the TypeScript runner produces frozen artifacts. The Python role is confined to analysis-side leverage, not runtime ownership.
keyPoints: Python notebooks are for post-experiment analysis only.
Analysis begins after stable JSON artifacts are written by the TypeScript runner.
Tasks include error clustering, confusion matrices, and long-tail pattern mining.
Prompt assembly and scoring remain in the TypeScript product path.
The goal is analysis-side leverage, not rewriting the runtime stack.
clusters.reason: All text captures are tightly related to the workflow of using Python for evaluation analysis after TypeScript runs. They share a common theme of separating runtime (TypeScript) from analysis (Python).

possibleTopics.reason: Directly matches the use of Python for analysis tasks like clustering after contracts are frozen.
Related to the evaluation and scoring workflow, though the captures focus on the Python analysis layer.

clusters.title: Pandas DataFrame.groupby documentation link
clusters.summary: A single link to the pandas documentation for the groupby method.
keyPoints: Link to pandas DataFrame.groupby API reference.
clusters.reason: This is a standalone reference link with no surrounding context. It could be related to the Python analysis work but is not substantive enough to archive on its own.
missingContext: Why was this link captured? What specific analysis task is it intended for?

Important output rules

3. Do not repeat sourceIds across clusters.
4. Assign every sourceId exactly once.

6. Include missingContext and possibleTopics arrays even when empty.
